posts.text: My TEDx "Ageism Loves Silence" hit a raw nerve. 7,200 views in 48 hours. But, ageism doesn't lack awareness. It lacks people willing to stop waiting for someone else to fix it. Most executives know the bias exists. They've felt it in interviews that went cold. In feedback that said "overqualified" but meant "too old." In rooms where the youngest voice gets the mic. They know. Because speaking up has a cost. It can follow you into the next interview. It can get you labeled "difficult." It can make you the example instead of the hire. So the silence compounds. If you wait for corporate to care, you won't be just be older, you'll be dead. Doing something about it before it's too late? That's the hard part. That many people watching a talk about getting fired at 55? Evidence the silence is breaking. Watch. Comment. Share. https://lnkd.in/e-AcXGsD
posts.hook: My TEDx "Ageism Loves Silence" hit a raw nerve.
posts.name: Hannah Morgan
